content: Population-level CVD prevention interventions: (1) Policy measures—sodium reduction targets (UK showing 15% population BP decrease), trans fat bans (Denmark, NYC associated with CVD mortality decline), sugar-sweetened beverage taxes, smoke-free legislation; (2) Food environment—healthy food procurement policies for institutions, front-of-package labeling, marketing restrictions, urban agriculture support; (3) Built environment—active transport infrastructure, park access, walkable communities; (4) Healthcare system—hypertension control programs ( Million Hearts initiative), statin therapy guidelines, diabetes prevention/management, aspirin use for secondary prevention; (5) Screening—blood pressure measurement in community settings, lipid screening, diabetes screening; (6) Medication access—generic antihypertensives, statins, fixed-dose polypills for high-risk populations. Community health worker programs improve control rates in underserved populations. Risk communication emphasizes absolute risk over relative risk.
